newsSince: The fresh item in the window is the announced departure of CFO Christian Mader at end-June 2026 (joining another Swiss industrial), with Andreas Weibel stepping in on an interim basis - a mild governance/execution overhang during an ongoing restructuring. No formal profit warning surfaced; the sharp ~-20% drop since June 9 in this micro-cap ($0.33B) looks largely sentiment/liquidity-driven amid weak European auto-wire demand. The CHF25M cost-cut and Asia localization plans continue.

sources.label: Komax CFO Christian Mader to leave (EQS News)
Komax CFO to depart at June-end (MarketScreener)
Komax reduces costs, positive operating result (Komax)

verdicts.rankRationale: Latent under-covered AI supply-chain lens: wire-processing automation thesis intact, but the end-June CFO departure adds a mild governance/execution overhang mid-restructuring. Price slide is handled by the separate re-rating penalty; small negative fundamental delta, no profit warning -> hold.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Wire-harness automation for robotics/power/industrial; CHF25M cost-out and Asia localization
verdicts.primaryRisk: CFO transition mid-restructuring; weak European auto-wire demand; micro-cap illiquidity
verdicts.bull: Niche wire-processing automation leader leveraged to robotics/power harness volumes once auto capex recovers.
verdicts.neutral: Restructuring and Asia push are credible but slow; CFO change adds near-term uncertainty.
verdicts.bear: Subdued auto-wire investment, -34% YTD, leadership churn and tiny float keep the strict screen fragile.
